entries.legacy_intent: FlowPilot treats the user contract as the acceptance floor and does not silently lower completion standards.
entries.next_action: Keep final-ledger source-of-truth and frozen-contract replay regressions in sync with future route surfaces.
entries.reason: This is not just an old prompt guard. It defines the acceptance floor and now has router-level final-ledger replay from the frozen root contract.

entries.legacy_intent: Controller relays envelopes, does not read sealed bodies, does not approve gates, and all formal role mail routes through Controller.
entries.next_action: Keep packet checks aligned as specialized report families grow.
entries.reason: This is the central anti-contamination mechanism of the new design and already has executable coverage for user intake and current-node packets.

entries.legacy_intent: Real FlowGuard must be importable; do not fake the toolchain; host-specific tools are capability mapped.
entries.next_action: Add capability-probe cards/events only when a route actually needs a host capability.
entries.reason: FlowGuard import is already checked, but host capability mapping is not yet expressed as system cards or router state.

entries.legacy_intent: FlowPilot activates only when explicitly requested; existing .flowpilot state, UI work, or heartbeat wording cannot auto-start it.
entries.next_action: Keep the small launcher test and check_install small-launcher check.
entries.reason: The small launcher already blocks implicit activation and prevents the old monolithic prompt from being read. A large startup reviewer gate is not needed for this specific risk.

entries.legacy_intent: Ask background-agent permission, scheduled-continuation permission, and display surface, then stop until explicit answers. The old run-mode question is retired.
entries.next_action: Keep the direct runtime regression that rejects banner/run-shell before explicit startup answers.
entries.reason: This is still necessary because it controls user consent for background agents, continuation, and display surface. It is not only an old-prompt contamination workaround.

entries.legacy_intent: Before work starts, reviewer and PM check current run, route/frontier, crew, continuation, prior-work boundary, and display surface evidence.
entries.next_action: Expand imported-artifact disposition when old files are intentionally imported; do not copy the whole old gate.
entries.reason: Some old checks can be downgraded because the small launcher prevents prompt overread. Real external authority checks are now split across startup fact-check, PM activation, continuation binding/quarantine, and crew freshness cards.

entries.legacy_intent: Full-auto, autonomous, guided, and strict-gated modes alter check-in behavior without lowering completion standards.
entries.next_action: Do not add a runtime mode card or router-visible mode state; keep hard gates and quality floor independent of any mode concept.
entries.reason: The prompt-isolated router no longer needs or exposes run modes. Asking fewer questions reduces startup ambiguity without lowering completion standards or hard gates.

entries.legacy_intent: The full old startup chain asks questions, creates run state, runs self-interrogation, creates crew, handles material, product architecture, route design, continuation, and route sign.
entries.next_action: Use this section as the main source for the next card batches: startup activation, material/research, product architecture, child-skill selection, and officer checks.
entries.reason: This section was an oversized orchestration prompt. In the new design it should be split across many phase cards and router transitions rather than kept as one card.

entries.legacy_intent: Inventory materials, have reviewer approve sufficiency, and hand reviewed material to PM before product design.
entries.next_action: Keep adding field-level validators as material and research schemas grow.
entries.reason: The new card chain now forces PM scan issue, sealed material packet relay, reviewer sufficiency, PM absorb-or-research, research package, research capability decision, sealed research packet/result relay, reviewer direct-source check, PM research absorption, and PM material understanding before product architecture.

entries.legacy_intent: PM writes product-function architecture before contract freeze and route design.
entries.next_action: Keep strengthening required product architecture fields as product-function modeling expands.
entries.reason: This prevents route work from starting from vague or implementation-shaped requirements. Runtime now requires PM material understanding, PM product architecture, product officer modelability, and reviewer challenge before root contract.

entries.legacy_intent: PM chooses child skills from actual task needs, not raw availability.
entries.next_action: Keep child-skill gate validators in sync with new child skills.
entries.reason: This is still needed because child skills can inject large prompt surfaces. The new prompt-isolation design now routes selection, gate extraction, reviewer challenge, officer conformance, and product-fit review before route activation.

entries.legacy_intent: Freeze root acceptance and scenario pack as the completion floor.
entries.legacy_section: Root Acceptance Contract And Standard Scenarios
entries.next_action: Keep frozen-contract replay fields aligned with root acceptance schema growth.
entries.reason: Final completion now requires source-of-truth replay for every frozen root requirement.

entries.legacy_intent: Each route node has explicit acceptance, visible route-sign obligations, PM high-standard recheck, parent backward targets, and replay conditions.
entries.next_action: Expand recursive route-tree and repair sibling traversal; PM high-standard recheck validation and route-display refresh are now router-backed.
entries.reason: The router now requires a reviewer-passed node acceptance plan, structured PM high-standard recheck, current-node packet registration, and parent backward replay before parent-node completion.

entries.legacy_intent: Protocol/tooling improvement notes are recorded separately and do not block project completion.
entries.next_action: Add nonblocking PM improvement-report card near closure.
entries.reason: This is useful but not needed before startup/material/route gates.

entries.legacy_intent: Track defects and evidence state; open blockers and stale evidence block terminal completion.
entries.next_action: Add explicit defect-ledger reconciliation to the closure suite.
entries.reason: Evidence, generated-resource, final-ledger, and residual-risk blockers are now bound into completion; explicit defect-ledger reconciliation remains to be added.

entries.legacy_intent: Use a fresh six-role crew per formal task; persist role memory; do not reuse old agent ids as current live roles.
entries.next_action: Expand live-agent replacement validation and role memory writeback after closure.
entries.reason: Even with smaller prompts, role authority depends on knowing which current role produced which decision. A crew rehydration/freshness card now covers resume/startup review; durable writeback remains an expansion.

entries.legacy_intent: Reviewer approvals require personal checking, not accepting worker/PM/controller summaries.
entries.next_action: Add reviewer cards for startup, material sufficiency, route review, final replay, and UI/display evidence.
entries.reason: The reviewer core card carries the main rule. More specialized cards are needed as new gate types are added.

entries.legacy_intent: Each gate has the correct actor; Controller is not PM/reviewer/officer/worker.
entries.next_action: Add explicit required_approver metadata to every gate artifact as schemas stabilize.
entries.reason: Authority is central to the new architecture. The router now enforces many role-owned writes directly, but not every artifact carries explicit approver metadata yet.

entries.legacy_intent: Approvers must probe failure hypotheses and cite concrete evidence before approving.
entries.next_action: Add approval-baseline snippets to reviewer/officer/PM gate cards.
entries.reason: The principle remains valid, but it should be delivered only to roles at approval points rather than kept in the launcher.

entries.legacy_intent: PM extracts and owns child-skill gate obligations before route work uses a child skill.
entries.next_action: Keep gate manifest schema tied to PM-selected skills.
entries.reason: Child skills are large prompt surfaces. The new architecture now requires PM selection and PM-owned gate extraction before reviewer and officer approval.

entries.legacy_intent: Reviewer/officer strict-gate review classifies caveats, blocks current-gate caveats, prevents PM override, routes repairs, invalidates stale evidence, and requires same-inspector recheck.
entries.next_action: Add caveat taxonomy fields, same-inspector recheck rule, and route activation validator.
entries.reason: This is a blocking approval rule, not merely a model output. A focused reviewer card now exists; router artifact validation is still the next step.

entries.legacy_intent: Interrogate understanding before freezing contract and route.
entries.next_action: Replace broad self-interrogation with a PM product-understanding challenge card after reviewed material.
entries.reason: The old visible self-interrogation was partly a monolithic prompt countermeasure, but product understanding still needs a structured PM challenge step.

entries.legacy_intent: Show a simple user-facing route/process sign in chat when Cockpit is unavailable, or in Cockpit when available; raw model graphs stay diagnostic.
entries.legacy_section: User Flow Diagram / Temporary Chat Cockpit
entries.next_action: Add native Cockpit consumption of the route snapshot; chat route-sign refresh is now recorded.
entries.reason: Cockpit-specific evidence can wait until the UI surface resumes, but the chat route sign is the active fallback display contract and now has runtime refresh metadata.

entries.legacy_intent: Use real FlowGuard for process design and product behavior modeling.
entries.next_action: Keep real-FlowGuard officer packets request-bound; add field-level validators as specialized model-report families grow.
entries.reason: Core role cards preserve the real-FlowGuard rule and the runtime now records PM-authorized officer request lifecycle.

entries.legacy_intent: Every meaningful scope has process and product/function models.
entries.next_action: Add specialized field-level validators as new model-report families appear.
entries.reason: The models exist for FlowPilot itself and the runtime router now records PM-authorized officer request lifecycle; report-specific validators remain future expansion.

entries.legacy_intent: PM may ask officers to model uncertain route, repair, feature, protocol, or validation decisions.
entries.next_action: Keep PM officer work packet-bound and add report-specific validators when needed.
entries.reason: This remains useful and should be delivered as bounded officer packets, not broad context. The PM lifecycle card and officer request lifecycle index now exist; specialized report fields remain next.

entries.legacy_intent: Reviewer observes artifacts personally, judges against contract, blocks concrete repair issues, and rechecks.
entries.next_action: Add reviewer observation/recheck cards for UI and product-behavior surfaces when those route types are active.
entries.reason: The current reviewer result card is packet-focused, and startup/final replay cards now exist. UI/product inspection remains route-surface-specific.

entries.legacy_intent: Plan route as a tree with parent/leaf nodes, recursive closure, and parent backward replay.
entries.next_action: Expand from effective-node auto-advance to full recursive tree and repair sibling resolution.
entries.reason: The router now auto-advances effective route nodes and gates parent closure on backward replay, but full recursive tree traversal and repair sibling resolution remain expansions.

entries.legacy_intent: If stopping before completion, record resume evidence and show the correct notice.
entries.next_action: Add Controller pause-snapshot action and PM stop/block decision path.
entries.reason: Resume reentry exists, but stop notices and pause snapshots are not yet router-driven.

entries.legacy_intent: Collect thin-scope review evidence, high-value improvement triage, child-skill mini-route visibility, validation-matrix sufficiency, anti-rough-finish review, and human-like inspection before checkpoint.
entries.next_action: Extend quality fields as new route surfaces require them.
entries.reason: Quality package is now PM-authored and reviewer-checked before final ledger work; stale evidence, unresolved evidence, pending resources, old UI assets, and completion-report-only closure are blocked.

entries.legacy_intent: Current run state lives under .flowpilot/runs/<run-id>; top-level files are pointers/catalog only.
entries.next_action: Expand old-file import disposition beyond the current quarantine baseline.
entries.reason: Startup creates run-local state, resume loads run-local state, continuation quarantine blocks old authority by default, and final ledgers are rebuilt from source-of-truth artifacts.

entries.legacy_intent: Use real scheduled continuation only when supported and authorized; otherwise record manual resume mode.
entries.next_action: Wire any host automation adapter to record the same one-minute binding event; do not create automation unless a formal run asks for it.
entries.reason: The user correctly noted this is still a real external capability check. The router now records one-minute host heartbeat bindings and heartbeat ticks, but host automation creation remains outside the router.

entries.legacy_intent: Choose child capabilities from route needs and evidence, preserve source-skill workflow fidelity, record loaded-file evidence, map gates/approvers, model conformance, run domain-quality review, and do not resume parent work until the child loop closes.
entries.next_action: Add source-skill loaded-file evidence, gate/approver mapping, domain-quality review, and child-loop closure validator.
entries.reason: Capability routing remains necessary but should be packetized and PM-owned. The current rebuild has PM selection/gate-manifest/officer cards and capability model checks; child-loop closure fidelity remains the main gap.

entries.legacy_intent: Do not load broad reference files into every actor; keep prompt layers bounded.
entries.next_action: Keep check_install enforcing small launcher and manifest card delivery.
entries.reason: This is the main architectural win of the rebuild. It no longer needs a large warning block in the launcher.

entries.legacy_intent: Old visual example guidance is an authenticity guard: old visuals must not be reused as current concept seeds or current evidence unless explicitly marked historical.
entries.next_action: Keep old visual assets quarantined as historical references only.
entries.reason: The PM evidence quality package now rejects old assets reused as current UI/visual proof.

entries.legacy_intent: UI/visual artifacts require source, authenticity, screenshot, and human-like review evidence.
entries.next_action: Add richer UI walkthrough fields when Cockpit/UI route work resumes.
entries.reason: UI/visual routes now require screenshot evidence and reject old-asset reuse before final ledger work. Richer walkthrough evidence remains route-surface-specific expansion.

entries.legacy_intent: PM rebuilds final ledger from current route, resolves stale/superseded/effective nodes, then reviewer performs terminal replay.
entries.next_action: Keep replay segment generation aligned with future source-of-truth entry types.
entries.reason: The router now writes a PM-owned source-of-truth final ledger, terminal replay map, segmented reviewer replay report, and terminal closure suite after PM closure.

entries.legacy_intent: Work is split into bounded chunks; chunks complete only after review and acceptance.
entries.next_action: Extend the same packet loop to repair packets and officer packets.
entries.reason: Current-node work is now bound to a reviewer-passed node acceptance plan and exact route version before worker dispatch.

entries.legacy_intent: Residual risks must be triaged before completion rather than hidden in the final report.
entries.next_action: Extend residual-risk source ledger if a route adds explicit risk entries.
entries.reason: The PM final ledger writer now rejects nonzero unresolved residual risk before terminal replay.

entries.legacy_intent: Terminal close reconciles defects, evidence, state, heartbeat/manual resume, role memory, and final report.
entries.next_action: Add explicit defect-ledger and role-memory reconciliation.
entries.reason: Closure is not only old-prompt protection; it prevents dangling automation/state/role memory. The router now writes the closure suite, disables continuation binding, blocks dirty evidence/resource/final ledgers, and records final user-report metadata; defect and role-memory reconciliation remain expansions.

entries.legacy_intent: New facts can mutate the route while preserving history and invalidating stale evidence.
entries.next_action: Expand route mutation from active repair nodes to full branch/sibling route replacement policies.
entries.reason: Route mutation now preserves superseded history, appends repair nodes, updates frontier, and writes stale-evidence records when backward review blocks continuation.

entries.legacy_intent: Do not silently bypass frozen acceptance, release, destructive operations, secrets, or completion standards.
entries.next_action: Add gate metadata to route/ledger artifacts and require PM/user approval where applicable.
entries.reason: Hard gates remain independent of prompt-size architecture.

entries.legacy_intent: Install only needed dependencies, defer route-specific dependencies, and avoid uncontrolled installs.
entries.next_action: Add router-visible dependency decision records when a route actually needs an install.
entries.reason: This is also a runtime dependency policy, not only a release/install concern. A PM dependency-policy card now captures the rule; install-time decision artifacts remain the next runtime expansion.

entries.legacy_intent: Completion requires evidence, final ledger, reviewer replay, PM approval, lifecycle cleanup, and clear user report.
entries.next_action: Keep closure authority separate from user-facing report output.
entries.reason: The final completion standard remains valid. The router now enforces PM evidence quality, PM final ledger, segmented reviewer terminal replay, PM closure, closure-suite lifecycle cleanup, and final user-report metadata while keeping the report output-only.

entries.legacy_intent: Old reference files explain protocol, installation, and failure modes.
entries.next_action: Keep references available for targeted maintainer/card-writing reads; launcher and runtime roles must not bulk-read them during activation.
entries.reason: References remain useful for maintainers and targeted card writing. The bulk-read prevention belongs to the prompt-layer-boundary rule, not to retiring the references.

scope: Map the second-backup monolithic FlowPilot prompt sections to the prompt-isolated router, runtime cards, validators, and intentional retirements.

defer_until_surface_exists: visible Cockpit evidence beyond the display-surface state contract
UI-specific route sign screenshot or interaction checks; chat route sign remains active when Cockpit is unavailable
downgrade_to_router_invariants: do not continue after asking startup questions
do not emit startup banner before answers
do not read broad FlowPilot references in the launcher
do not start work without a router pending action
keep_as_hard_checks: fresh current run root and current/index pointer correctness
same-task six-role crew freshness or authorized fallback
continuation mode evidence: real heartbeat if authorized, manual resume if not
display-surface evidence: Cockpit or chat route sign according to the user's answer
old state, old assets, and old agent ids quarantined from current authority
retire_as_old_architecture_guard: large startup prose that existed only because the old SKILL.md loaded all downstream rules at once
startup_hard_gate_reduction.summary: The old startup hard gate should not be copied wholesale. In the prompt-isolated architecture, the main anti-overread protections move to the small launcher, router pending actions, manifest checks, packet ledger checks, and Controller role cards.
